title: alcea rufescens
intro: Alcea rufescens, also known as the red hollyhock or hollyhock, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family that is native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Alcea rufescens include its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with colds and respiratory infections. Alcea rufescens can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal properties, and it is also used as a topical treatment for skin conditions such as wounds and eczema.
